req.title: Rate limiting
req.statement: The API shall enforce 60 requests per minute per user; overages shall return 429 with retry-after.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-024 mandates 60 req/min per-user rate limiting with HTTP 429 + Retry-After on overage. Three orthogonal cases are needed: (1) EP/positive – 59 requests within a minute all succeed (inside the allowed partition); (2) BVA/boundary – the 60th request succeeds and the 61st is the first rejected request, probing both edges of the limit; (3) NEG/negative – a burst well above 60 immediately triggers 429 with a valid Retry-After header, verifying the error pathway. Each test resets state via a unique per-test user token so they remain independent and idempotent. A <reset_rate_limit> fixture provides an admin call to flush counters before each test, ensuring determinism.

tests.name: test_requests_within_limit_all_succeed
preconditions: Valid bearer token for a test user is available
Rate-limit counter for this user has been reset to 0
actions: Send 59 GET /health requests sequentially under the same user token within a 60-second window
Collect all HTTP response status codes
trace: R-FIN-024: requests within the 60 req/min quota must succeed
EP: 59 requests belong to the valid partition (1-60 inclusive)

tests.name: test_60th_request_succeeds_and_61st_is_rejected
preconditions: Valid bearer token for a test user is available
Rate-limit counter for this user has been reset to 0
actions: Send exactly 60 GET /health requests under the same user token
Send one additional (61st) GET /health request immediately
Inspect response status code and Retry-After header
expected: Response 61 contains a Retry-After header with a positive integer value
trace: R-FIN-024: the 60th request is the last allowed; 61st is first overage
BVA: tests the boundary at exactly N=60 (max) and N=61 (min overage)

tests.name: test_burst_above_limit_returns_429_with_retry_after
preconditions: Valid bearer token for a test user is available
Rate-limit counter for this user has been reset to 0
actions: Send 80 GET /health requests in rapid succession under the same user token
Identify the first response with status 429
Verify Retry-After header on all 429 responses
expected: Every 429 response includes a Retry-After header
Retry-After header value is a positive integer (seconds until window resets)
trace: R-FIN-024: overages shall return 429 with retry-after
NEG: exercises the error/rejection pathway when requests exceed the quota
R-FIN-024: request tail rejected error pathway
